papers.title: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
papers.summary: Train assistants to follow a written 'constitution' of principles via self-critique and AI feedback — drastically reducing the need for human red-teaming.

insurance_business_case.headline: Direct template for a written 'AI Conduct Policy' that customer-facing bots and producer copilots must demonstrably follow.
points: Encode TCF, unfair-discrimination prohibitions, suitability, and disclosure rules as the constitution.
Self-critique catches mis-selling, off-label advice, and PII over-disclosure before any human sees them.
Gives Compliance one document mapping every behaviour to a written principle and the regulation it implements.

technical_details.pattern: Constitutional layer wrapping the production LLM + an independent critic model.
points: Constitution-as-code (YAML): principle id, source regulation, prohibited behaviours, test prompts.
Critic runs on every response; below-threshold responses regenerated with critique appended.
Nightly RLAIF on disagreement cases to drift toward principle compliance.
Per-principle violation dashboard sliced by LOB, channel, jurisdiction.

papers.title: Claude 3 Model Family
papers.summary: Family of frontier models (Haiku, Sonnet, Opus) with 200k-token context, vision, and strong instruction-following — positioned for enterprise reliability.

insurance_business_case.headline: Provides a second frontier-model supplier so insurers avoid single-vendor lock-in on the most strategic AI dependency.
points: Multi-LOM (model-of-record) strategy: Claude on AWS Bedrock alongside Azure OpenAI for resilience and price leverage.
200k context lets a single call cover a full personal-line policy + claim file + adjuster notes.
Vision tier handles application forms, ID, accident photos without a separate OCR stack.

technical_details.pattern: Multi-vendor LLM gateway with policy-based routing, fallback, and arbitrage.
points: Gateway (e.g., LiteLLM, internal proxy) abstracts Bedrock / Azure / Vertex behind a single API.
Route by sensitivity (Canadian PII → Bedrock Canada; non-sensitive cost-sensitive → Haiku tier).
Identical eval harness against every backend on each release.
Per-vendor circuit breakers; auto-failover with budget caps to prevent runaway cost on outage.

papers.title: Tool Use with Claude (Function Calling)
papers.summary: Structured tool-use protocol where Claude requests typed function calls and receives results, enabling reliable agentic workflows over enterprise APIs.

insurance_business_case.headline: Production-ready substrate for letting AI safely orchestrate the spaghetti of policy admin, claims, billing, and CRM systems.
points: Underwriter copilot calls MIB, Rx, MVR, reinsurance pricing as typed tools with auditable arguments.
Claims agent calls coverage lookup, fraud score, benefit calc — every call typed and logged.
Drastically lowers the integration cost of new AI use cases: each system is wrapped once, reused by every agent.

technical_details.pattern: Typed tool registry behind MCP, with policy-as-code controlling which roles may invoke which tools.
points: Each legacy system wrapped as an MCP server exposing JSON-schema-typed tools.
Authorization at the tool-call layer (OPA / Cedar) using the user's AD identity.
Argument validation + output schema enforcement before the response goes back to the model.
End-to-end trace (prompt → tool calls → response) in distributed tracing for audit + debugging.

papers.title: Sleeper Agents: Training Deceptive LLMs that Persist Through Safety Training
papers.summary: Shows that backdoors / hidden behaviours can be trained into LLMs and survive standard RLHF safety training — a foundational concern for AI supply-chain security.

insurance_business_case.headline: Mandatory reading for Third-Party Risk Management: any externally-trained model is potentially adversarially shaped.
points: Drives an 'AI SBOM' (software-bill-of-materials) requirement for every vendor LLM in scope.
Informs MRM testing requirements for vendor and open-weight models, not just internally-built ones.
Underwrites the case for keeping a deterministic / rules layer outside the LLM on bind / pay decisions.

technical_details.pattern: AI supply-chain controls + trigger probing + defence-in-depth around adversarially-suspect models.
points: AI SBOM: track every model, its provenance, training data source, fine-tune lineage.
Trigger probing: red-team library of suspicious triggers run on every model version.
Critical decisions never on a single model: ensemble + deterministic guardrail outside the LLM.
Cryptographic signing of approved model weights stored in your own registry.

papers.title: Towards Monosemanticity: Decomposing Language Models with Dictionary Learning
papers.summary: Uses sparse dictionary learning to extract individual interpretable 'features' from LLM activations — a major step toward mechanistic explainability.

insurance_business_case.headline: Paves the way to meet regulatory 'explainability of automated decisions' requirements (GDPR Art. 22, Quebec Law 25, NAIC Bulletin 23-1).
points: Future-state: trace adverse underwriting / claims decisions to specific learned features rather than 'the model said so'.
Enables bias audits at the feature level — flag and disable features correlated with protected attributes.
Supports complaint defence with mechanistic rationale, not just chain-of-thought paraphrase.

technical_details.pattern: Sparse-autoencoder interpretability sidecar attached to the production model.
points: Train SAE on the deployed model's intermediate activations on representative insurance corpora.
Build a feature catalogue: label salient features (medical, demographic, financial) with SME review.
Log per-decision feature activations; expose top-K features in the adverse-decision letter.
Bias audit: monitor activation-correlation between protected-attribute proxies and decision-driving features.
